text: Between 1951 and 1991 seventeen separate uranium processes were developed from wet phosphoric acid (WPA) uranium recovery technology. The applicability and economic viability of most of these processes have varied with uranium prices. The interest in uranium recovery from wet phosphoric acid is increasing due to the world uranium demand, and the rapid growth of nuclear energy program in developing countries. This has stimulated the optimisation of previous processes as well as the research of new extraction methods. The present paper reviews the various historical uranium processes and their underlying physico-chemistry as well as the more recent developments in new extraction processes.
